name: William J. Cosgrove
title: Curriculum Designer, Educator, and Researcher
summary: Curriculum designer and learning systems thinker with graduate-level training in instructional design, AI-integrated pedagogy, and equity-centered education. Completing MA at George Washington University with thesis arguing K-12 education should be reorganized around metacognition rather than prescribed content.

thesis.title: Same Means, New End: A Metacognitive End for American Curriculum
thesis.argument: K-12 education should be reorganized around how students think (metacognition) rather than prescribed content.

keyConcepts.contextures: Interwoven learning environments that bring together themes, tasks, and sources so students work on how they learn, not just what they learn.
keyConcepts.cogniscence: Learning that includes awareness of how it is changing what you know and how you are able to know.
keyConcepts.axiometricCalculus: A formal notation system for metacognitive operations grounded in the causal architecture of reality.

projects.name: Gamified U.S. History Simulation
projects.description: Partnered with GW's Open-Source Program Office, CS faculty, and digital artists to design a gamified U.S. history curriculum using role-play, decision-making, and narrative strategy.

projects.name: AI in Civics Curriculum
projects.description: Lesson series helping high school students critically evaluate generative AI tools for civic understanding, source reliability, and digital thinking skills.

projects.name: Mastering GenAI for Learning Design
projects.description: Self-paced instructional module helping educators generate AI-assisted lesson plans. Published on a responsive, no-login web platform.

projects.name: QGen
projects.description: Company-wide project management and survey analysis platform. Automates questionnaire construction, SPSS data validation, incidence rate calculation, and crosstab generation.

projects.name: The R3 Model
projects.description: Rapid instructional design model balancing GenAI integration with equity, adaptability, and user-centered testing.

education.degree: MA in Curriculum Design and Pedagogy
honors: Graduate Certificate in Curriculum Writing, Evaluation, and Development

education.degree: BA in History and Secondary Education (Dual Major)

credentials: DC Educator Credential (OSSE) - Active through May 2029
Praxis: Social Studies Content Knowledge - Passed
Praxis: Principles of Learning and Teaching - Passed

aiPhilosophy.summary: AI is a tool, not a replacement for thinking. School district AI policies tend to be defensive rather than reimagining curriculum. The historical pattern with technology in education follows the same arc: panic, prohibition, integration, redesign. AI should accelerate the move toward metacognition-centered learning.
keyPositions: AI does not think, understand, or create - it processes, predicts, and generates
Prompt engineering is a design discipline, not a shortcut
AI should accelerate the move away from content-first schooling
Self-taught in Python, AI tooling, and development
Built production tools: QGen, AI-driven qualitative coding, R3 instructional design model

values: Know Your Students - Make students feel like people, understand their lives, ensure safe learning environments
Representation Matters - Students need to see themselves in the curriculum, live it, feel connected to it
Adapt Always - Learn to use new educational technologies thoughtfully, integrate tools like AI into practice, flexibility is essential
